zh.lifeSummary: 當George W York於1596年出生在英國時，他的父親George Yorke33歲，母親Sarah Frances Astyll30歲。他於1614年11月2日與Ellen Losbye在英國萊希斯特斯爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1664年11月4日在英國諾薩普頓史爾納希比享年68歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾米德萊頓 切尼哥吉拉 沙智 亞德。

nameMeanings.name: York
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from the city of York in northern England. The surname is now widespread throughout England. Originally, the city bore the Latin name Eburacum, which is probably from a Brittonic name meaning ‘yew-tree place’. This was altered by folk etymology to Old English Eoforwīc (from the elements eofor ‘wild boar’ + wīc ‘specialized farmstead’). This name was taken over by Scandinavian settlers, who altered it back to opacity in the form Jórvík or Jórk (English York, which became finally settled as the placename in the 13th century). The surname has also been adopted by Jews as an Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish surnames.
In some cases also an American shortened and altered form of the East Slavic patronymic   Yurkovich   or its Croatian, Slovak, or Slovenian variants. Compare   Yurk  .

variants.name: Yurkovich
meanings: Ukrainian (standard transliteration Yurkovych) and Belorussian: patronymic from the personal name Yurko and its Belorussian equivalent Yurka, pet forms of Ukrainian Yuriy and Belorussian Yuray, equivalents of Greek Geōrgios (see George ).
Americanized form of Croatian Jurković, Slovak and Slovenian Jurkovič (see Jurkovic , compare Jurkovich and Yurkovic ).
Jewish (from Bessarabia): habitational name for someone from Yurkovtsy in Ukraine.

nameMeanings.name: George
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When George W York was born in 1596, in England, his father, George Yorke, was 33 and his mother, Sarah Frances Astyll, was 30. He married Ellen Losbye on 2 November 1614, in Leicestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 4 November 1664, in Naseby, Northamptonshire, England, at the age of 68, and was buried in All Saints Churchyard, Middleton Cheney, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom.

ko.All Saints Churchyard, Naseby, Daventry: 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 미들레턴 천이 앨 세인츠 c후르c히아어드
ko.lifeSummary: George W York(이)가 1596년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 George Yorke(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Sarah Frances Astyll(은)는 30세였다. 그는 1614년 11월 2일 영국 레이스스터실르에서 Ellen Losbye(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1664년 11월 4일 영국 노르섬프토실르 나세비에서 68세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 미들레턴 천이 앨 세인츠 c후르c히아어드에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда George W York родился в 1596 г. в местности Англия, его отцу по имени George Yorke было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Sarah Frances Astyll, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Ellen Losbye 2 ноября 1614 г. в местности лисестиршир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер 4 ноября 1664 г. в местности неисеби, нортамптоншир, Англия в возрасте 68 лет и был похоронен в местности алл сеинц чурчярд, миддлетон чэни, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания.
